title: Sullivan & Cromwell apologises to US court for filing errors caused by AI hallucinations
description: Sullivan & Cromwell LLP has apologised to a US bankruptcy court after admitting that a filing in ongoing insolvency proceedings contained inaccurate citations generated by artificial intelligence (AI) tools.In a letter to Chief Judge Martin Glenn of the US Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of New York, the law firm said that an emergency motion filed earlier in the matter of In re Prince Global Holdings Limited contained “inaccurate citations and other errors.”The firm acknowledged
